commands.review_summary:
The branch classifies Telegram Bot API 5xx responses as recoverable outside send context, adds classifier regression tests, and documents Bad Gateway polling/startup troubleshooting.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +14, Docs +1. Total +19 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main converts getUpdates 502 responses into errors with error_code, then the isolated worker exits if the classifier does not treat them as recoverable. I did not induce a live Telegram 502 during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the reviewed branch is already a focused implementation and no actionable patch defect was found.

target.title: [codex] recover Telegram polling from Bot API 5xx
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

A transient Telegram Bot API 502 from `getUpdates` can cross the isolated polling worker boundary as a plain Bot API server error. `isRecoverableTelegramNetworkError` recognized transport/network-shaped failures, but not Telegram server 5xx responses, so polling treated the response as fatal. The worker exited, the outer channel supervisor backed off, and the gateway could remain healthy while Telegram appeared unresponsive.

The fix classifies Telegram Bot API HTTP 5xx responses as recoverable for polling/setup/control-plane contexts while keeping `send` context conservative so non-idempotent visible messages are not retried just because Telegram returned 5xx.

## Summary

- classify Telegram Bot API HTTP 5xx responses as recoverable for polling/setup/control-plane contexts
- keep `send` context conservative to avoid duplicate visible messages
- document the `Bad Gateway` troubleshooting behavior for Telegram polling/startup instability

## Evidence

- Focused regression: `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/network-errors.test.ts` passes: 53 tests
- Docs formatting: `pnpm format:docs:check` passes: docs formatting clean
- Local observed failure pattern: gateway health remained live, but Telegram logs repeated `Bad Gateway | Telegram ingress worker exited with code 1` after Bot API timeouts/502s
- Local proof after applying the same classifier hotpatch: safe gateway restart, Telegram account connected, and `openclaw message send --channel telegram ... --json` returned `ok: true` with Telegram message id `7200`
- Local `pnpm check:changed` did not execute checks because the delegated Blacksmith Testbox `crabbox` binary failed its own sanity check before running
target.author: ghitafilali
